name: whois
purpose: Domain and IP registration data lookup
author: Ken Harrenstien & Vic White (RFC 812); Marco d'Itri (modern Linux CLI)
why_notable: whois was born on ARPANET in 1982 as a hand-maintained text file of every machine on the early internet, run by Elizabeth 'Jake' Feinler at SRI-NIC. The RFC 812 protocol — connect to TCP port 43, send a query, get text back — has barely changed in over 40 years. Today the Linux CLI (maintained by Marco d'Itri) auto-routes queries to the correct RIR or registrar. GDPR (2018) largely redacted registrant personal data, so modern value lies in registrar name, creation/expiry dates, nameservers, and status flags. It's the first tool in any recon chain: it tells you who controls DNS before you send a single packet to the target.
